general_goal_of_analysis: Test the hypothesis that capital flow does not influence productive investment in Brazil, and analyze the determinants of productivity conditioned on investment rates, wage costs, and external demand
instrumental_variable_instrument: Not explicitly named in abstract; instruments used to address endogeneity between productivity, investment rates, and wages in a 2SLS and GMM framework
main_variable_relationship: Effect of investment rate on productivity, and effect of capital flow on productive investment
statement_of_identification_assumptions_: A relação entre a produtividade e às taxas de investimentos e salários sugere uma possível simultaneidade, dada a natureza distributiva da renda agregada entre capital e trabalho. Portanto, são utilizados estimadores que tratam a endogeneidade em dois estágios
brief_justification: Empirical macroeconomics paper using time-series data for Brazil with 2SLS and GMM estimators to address endogeneity between productivity and investment/wage variables. Tests a new-developmentalist hypothesis that foreign savings (capital flow) does not promote productive investment. The paper specifies econometric equations, acknowledges simultaneity bias, and uses instrumental variable methods, though the specific instruments and sample size are not detailed in the available metadata. Published in a political economy journal with a Kaldorian/post-Keynesian theoretical framework.
